purpose: Reference documentation for all commands in {tracker_root}/_commands/project/. Documents project setup commands (!initialize, !onboard, !scaffold, !create_claude), analysis commands (!context, !review, !health, !plan, !scope, !trace, !contracts, !env_check), TOC commands (!toc, !toc_generate, !toc_update), PKI commands (!learn, !learn_update), instrumentation (!instrument), audit (!prompt_audit), and discovery (!commands, !profiles, !help, !guide). Most are read-only and run in serial mode.
gotchas: Most project commands are read-only by design -- they do not modify project files
!create_claude vs !scaffold: scaffold documents what IS, create_claude prescribes what SHOULD BE
!learn requires no PKI; !learn_update requires existing manifest.json
!toc_generate dispatches parallel agents -- master never reads source files directly
patterns: Each command documented with Purpose / Syntax / Key Behavior
Commands grouped by lifecycle (setup -> analysis -> TOC -> PKI -> audit -> discovery)
Parallel dispatch commands (!toc_generate, !learn) use !p mode internally
conventions: Read-only by default; modifying commands explicitly stated
Serial mode default; parallel mode noted explicitly
Idempotency for commands like !initialize that may be re-run
